name: Use of Single-factor Authentication
description: The product uses single-factor authentication (e.g., password only) in a security context that warrants multi-factor authentication. If the single factor is compromised, the attacker gains complete access with no additional barriers.
detection_pattern: Look for AUTH nodes (authentication decision, login gate) that accept only one INGRESS factor (password, PIN, single token) without a secondary AUTH mediator (second factor check, MFA verification, out-of-band confirmation) that requires an independent additional proof of identity before granting access to sensitive resources.
